response:
Sec. 1  
people who are allergic to peanuts cannot live underwater because their immune system reacts to peanut proteins which can cause severe allergic reactions such as anaphylaxis which is life-threatening and cannot be managed in an underwater environment where medical help is not readily available and emergency interventions are difficult to perform  

Sec. 2  
furthermore living underwater requires adaptation to environments that are not suitable for human physiology and the presence of allergens in such environments would pose an additional risk making it impossible for individuals with peanut allergies to survive safely in underwater habitats without extensive precautions which are not feasible in practice
